sequences.behavioral_trigger: Subscriber engages with this week's case drop or preview interest for the new weekly case release

emails.subject_line_a: A miracle drug. A dead scientist. Tonight's case.
emails.subject_line_b: Skip movie night. Open this case instead.
emails.preheader: A biotech breakthrough, a staged accident, and a story that feels too tidy.
emails.body_markdown:
If tonight was heading toward another round of scrolling, here is a better plan.

This week's case is **The Miracle Withdrawal** - a suspenseful investigation set inside a biotech company in Cambridge, where a breakthrough drug appears to cure a chronic disease... and then everything goes quiet.

The lead scientist, **Dr. Mara Ellison**, is found dead after what looks like a workplace accident. But the official version lands a little too neatly. The building is all badge access, sensors, logs, locked doors, and carefully managed stories. Which is exactly what makes the case so unsettling: in a place built to record everything, something still does not add up.

The opening file drops you into that first feeling of unease. A miracle. A death. A company pulling the drug before anyone can ask why it worked so well.

Inside the full case, you will work through **4 envelopes**, follow a trail across **21 documents**, and piece together what happened over a **2-4 hour** investigation. It works for **1-6 players**, so yes - it absolutely works as a sharp, adult-feeling night for two.

No awkward role-play. No shipping wait. Just a case file you can open tonight.

If you want a date night with a plot, start here.
emails.pain_point_addressed: We keep defaulting to Netflix, and it doesn't feel like we're actually doing something together.
emails.fear_neutralized: What if this is boring or cringe once we start?
emails.compliance_notes: Uses only verified claims for price-adjacent value, play time, player count, document count from weekly case brief, and instant access. Does not reveal culprit or imply real case.
emails.tone: inviting, narrative, and conversion-focused without sounding pushy

emails.subject_line_a: What is actually inside this week's case?
emails.subject_line_b: A group mystery night, mapped out for you
emails.preheader: See the suspects, the structure, and why this one works for 3-6 players.
emails.body_markdown:
Here is what your group would actually be opening.

**The Miracle Withdrawal** begins in **Kendall Square, Cambridge**, inside Helixure Biologics - a building of immaculate labs, controlled access points, and a stairwell death that looks staged from the moment you start reading.

This is not a vague puzzle pack. It is a structured investigation with **4 envelopes** and **21 documents** designed to pull people into the same theory-building conversation fast.

You will examine the death of **Dr. Mara Ellison**, a principal research scientist, and work through a suspect field that includes:

- **Evan Kline**, the CFO with financial pressure hanging over the drug's future
- **Nadia Reyes**, Ellison's research associate with late-night access
- **Tom Briggs**, the facilities and security manager tied to the building systems
- **Raina Patel**, the QA director whose signatures sit close to the rollout

What makes this case work especially well for a small group is the format: timelines, evidence, system clues, and suspect motives that naturally give everyone something to latch onto. No host script. No costumes. No long rules talk before anyone cares.

You just download the file, open the first envelope, and start arguing over what the logs, scene details, and conflicting motives really mean.

If your usual rotation needs something fresher than another board game, this is your anchor activity.
emails.pain_point_addressed: If a group activity takes forever to explain, people check out before we even start.
emails.fear_neutralized: I do not want something complicated to set up.
emails.compliance_notes: Uses case facts from weekly brief and verified product facts only. Avoids fabricated social proof, guarantee, and unsupported comparative claims.
emails.tone: inviting, narrative, and conversion-focused without sounding pushy

emails.subject_line_b: One last nudge to open the file tonight
emails.preheader: A staged fall, silenced alarms, and a case built for one sharp night in.
emails.body_markdown:
This is your final nudge on **The Miracle Withdrawal**.

If you are the kind of person who keeps mentally rewriting the ending of every true-crime episode, this case was built for that exact mood. The setup alone does the job: a scientist dead in a controlled biotech facility, a miracle drug abruptly pulled, and clues suggesting the "no alarms" story may have been authored by someone who understood the building far too well.

And that is what makes this one hard to shake.

The intrigue runs through the details:

- a stairwell in a high-control facility that should not have gone unobserved
- a motive map crowded with money, audit pressure, access, and blame
- the possibility that official documentation was shaping the story in real time

You can start tonight with **instant access after purchase**. The experience runs **2-4 hours**, works for **1-6 players**, and gives you a complete case in one sitting - no shipping, no subscription, no waiting around for the next installment.

If streaming has started to feel too passive, this is the better solo plan: one file, one night, one investigation that asks you to actually connect the evidence.

Open the first envelope while the mood is still here.
emails.pain_point_addressed: I love true crime, but most nights I'm just passively watching instead of doing something engaging.
emails.fear_neutralized: I do not want to wait for shipping.
emails.compliance_notes: Uses verified claims only: instant access, 2-4 hours, 1-6 players, one-case standalone framing. No fabricated scarcity, testimonials, or guarantees.
emails.tone: inviting, narrative, and conversion-focused without sounding pushy

sequences.behavioral_trigger: New subscriber opts in for the free mini-case preview: Case File Preview: The First Envelope

emails.preheader: A quick look inside the case format, without waiting for a box or subscription.
emails.body_markdown:
Here it is: your **Case File Preview: The First Envelope**.

You signed up because the category is intriguing - but you probably also know the friction that usually comes with it. Shipping delays. Bigger price tags. Subscription models that turn one good mystery night into an ongoing commitment.

This preview is meant to answer the first question fast: **what does this actually feel like?**

Declassified Cases is built as a **digital download** you can access instantly after purchase. Each full case is a complete **2-4 hour** investigation with **4 envelopes**, **14-21 documents per case**, and play designed for **1-6 players**.

In other words: one case, one night, no recurring box showing up later.

As you go through the preview, pay attention to the structure and atmosphere - the dossier feel, the evidence flow, and the way the investigation unfolds. That is the core experience we want people to understand before they buy.

If you have ever wanted the satisfaction of a mystery night without the shipping wait or subscription fatigue, this is the simplest way to see how it works.

When you are ready, the full cases are waiting on the site.
emails.pain_point_addressed: I like mystery boxes, but I do not want another subscription to manage.
emails.fear_neutralized: I do not want something complicated to set up.
emails.compliance_notes: Aligned to lead magnet and verified facts. Avoids unsupported release cadence, guarantee, and review claims.
emails.tone: inviting, narrative, and conversion-focused without sounding pushy

emails.subject_line_a: How the full case turns into date night
emails.subject_line_b: What happens after the first envelope
emails.preheader: From preview to full investigation: a better night in, without overplanning.
emails.body_markdown:
If the preview gave you the feeling, here is what the full experience adds.

A complete Declassified Cases file is designed to turn an ordinary night at home into a shared investigation. You are not learning a complicated game system or acting out roles. You are opening documents, comparing theories, and moving through a story together.

Each full case includes **4 envelopes** and **14-21 documents**, with a total play time of about **2-4 hours**. It works for **1-6 players**, but one of the strongest fits is two people who want something more memorable than another default movie night.

That matters if your usual problem is not finding entertainment - it is finding something that actually feels shared.

This is especially useful when the plan comes together late. Because the format is **100% digital** with **instant access after purchase**, you can decide tonight and still start tonight.

The product is priced at **$19.99 per case**, which makes it an easy way to upgrade the evening without turning it into a whole outing.

If you were hoping the preview would show something more polished than a generic printable, the next step is simple: pick a case and open the full file.
emails.pain_point_addressed: We want something we can start tonight without waiting for shipping or learning a bunch of rules first.
emails.fear_neutralized: I am worried this will feel childish, fake, or unrealistic.
emails.compliance_notes: Uses only verified product facts and approved pricing. No fabricated testimonials or exaggerated quality claims.
emails.tone: inviting, narrative, and conversion-focused without sounding pushy

emails.subject_line_a: A thoughtful gift you can send tonight
emails.subject_line_b: Last-minute does not have to feel lazy
emails.preheader: A digital mystery gift for true-crime fans, couples, or small groups.
emails.body_markdown:
If you downloaded the preview and immediately thought, *someone I know would love this*, that instinct is probably right.

Declassified Cases works especially well as a practical experience gift because it removes the usual last-minute gifting problems. There is **instant access after purchase**, no shipping delay, and the format is flexible enough to fit different kinds of recipients.

A full case can be played **solo**, as a **couple**, or with a **small group of up to 6**, which lowers the risk of gifting something too niche or too gamer-specific. The appeal is not heavy mechanics - it is the feeling of opening a file, following evidence, and spending **2-4 hours** inside a smart mystery.

At **$19.99 per case**, it also lands in that useful range where it feels more intentional than a gift card without becoming another object someone has to store.

If you need a birthday, anniversary, or same-day surprise that feels distinctive, this is one of the cleanest ways to give an experience instead of more stuff.

Send the case while the idea is fresh.
emails.pain_point_addressed: I always end up panic-buying something generic because I remember gifts too late.
emails.fear_neutralized: If I give this as a gift, what if they do not like it?
emails.compliance_notes: Gift framing uses verified flexibility, price, and instant access only. No guarantee or fabricated recipient satisfaction claims.
emails.tone: inviting, narrative, and conversion-focused without sounding pushy

narrative_arc: The weekly case sequence moves from intrigue to clarity to action: first, it teases the unsettling premise of a miracle drug and a staged death; second, it reveals the suspect map, structure, and investigative format; third, it pushes the reader to act while the case mood is still vivid. The welcome sequence follows a trust-building arc from preview delivery to product understanding to a use-case-based buy decision.
optimization_applied: OPT-W14-002: Increased product clarity by showing what the buyer receives through specific mentions of envelopes, document count, suspects, and investigative structure.
Addressed LP-001 by reducing ambiguity around the product format and emphasizing instant-access digital case details in every email.
